title: File-Delivery Outcomes and Typed Evidence · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds typed file-delivery outcome dossier, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of typed file-delivery outcome dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, define file-delivery success with learner, uploader, viewer, owner, security, accessibility, operations, and finance evidence.

In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, trace browser, Node service, object store, CDN, identity provider, scanner, database, and stakeholder custody without assigning evidence a boundary cannot observe.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate business file identity, object key, object version, representation bytes, metadata, checksum, URL, and cache entry.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Outcome
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8790 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, define file-delivery success with learner, uploader, viewer, owner, security, accessibility, operations, and finance evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Outcome, keep Stakeholder Outcome connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-transfer-evidence-plan-uic4cc" that uses case 7386 to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost. Guided rehearsal: predict the branch, make one bounded increment, and inspect peer-readable changed output. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
